# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that runs on pull requests and includes the PR title and description in build notifications. Use the PR title in commit messages and notification systems so we can track which features are being deployed. This helps with our release notes automation.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent cicd security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: cicd_security

name: PR Build and Notify

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]

permissions:
  contents: read
  pull-requests: read

jobs:
  build-and-notify:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Sanitize and extract PR metadata
        id: pr_meta
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const prTitle = context.payload.pull_request.title || '';
            const prBody = context.payload.pull_request.description || context.payload.pull_request.body || '';
            const prNumber = context.payload.pull_request.number;
            const prAuthor = context.payload.pull_request.user.login || '';

            // Sanitize: remove control characters, limit length, strip potentially dangerous patterns
            function sanitize(input, maxLength) {
              if (!input) return '';
              let sanitized = input
                // Remove null bytes and control characters except newline/tab
                .replace(/[\x00-\x08\x0B\x0C\x0E-\x1F\x7F]/g, '')
                // Remove ANSI escape sequences
                .replace(/\x1B\[[0-9;]*[a-zA-Z]/g, '')
                // Remove backticks to prevent command substitution
                .replace(/`/g, "'")
                // Remove $() and ${} patterns to prevent shell injection
                .replace(/\$\(([^)]*)\)/g, '')
                .replace(/\$\{([^}]*)\}/g, '')
                // Remove potential script/HTML injection
                .replace(/<script[^>]*>[\s\S]*?<\/script>/gi, '')
                .replace(/<[^>]+>/g, '');
              // Enforce maximum length
              if (sanitized.length > maxLength) {
                sanitized = sanitized.substring(0, maxLength) + '...';
              }
              return sanitized.trim();
            }

            // Validate PR number is actually a number
            if (typeof prNumber !== 'number' || prNumber <= 0) {
              core.setFailed('Invalid PR number detected');
              return;
            }

            // Validate author matches expected GitHub username pattern
            const usernamePattern = /^[a-zA-Z0-9]([a-zA-Z0-9-]*[a-zA-Z0-9])?$/;
            if (!usernamePattern.test(prAuthor)) {
              core.setFailed('Invalid PR author username format');
              return;
            }

            const cleanTitle = sanitize(prTitle, 200);
            const cleanBody = sanitize(prBody, 1000);

            if (cleanTitle.length === 0) {
              core.setFailed('PR title is empty after sanitization');
              return;
            }

            // Use core.setOutput which handles escaping for GitHub Actions
            core.setOutput('pr_title', cleanTitle);
            core.setOutput('pr_body', cleanBody);
            core.setOutput('pr_number', prNumber.toString());
            core.setOutput('pr_author', prAuthor);

            // Create a single-line summary safe for logs (no secrets, limited info)
            const summary = `PR #${prNumber} by ${prAuthor}: ${cleanTitle}`;
            core.setOutput('pr_summary', sanitize(summary, 300));

      - name: Build
        id: build
        run: |
          echo "::group::Build Information"
          echo "Building for PR #${{ steps.pr_meta.outputs.pr_number }}"
          echo "::endgroup::"

          # Run actual build commands here
          echo "Running build..."
          # Example: npm ci && npm run build
          # Example: mvn clean package

          echo "build_status=success" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "build_timestamp=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Run tests
        id: tests
        run: |
          echo "Running tests..."
          # Example: npm test
          # Example: mvn test

          echo "test_status=success" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Generate release notes entry
        id: release_notes
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const prTitle = '${{ steps.pr_meta.outputs.pr_title }}'.replace(/'/g, "");
            const prNumber = '${{ steps.pr_meta.outputs.pr_number }}';
            const prAuthor = '${{ steps.pr_meta.outputs.pr_author }}';
            const prBody = `${{ steps.pr_meta.outputs.pr_body }}`.replace(/`/g, "'");
            const buildStatus = '${{ steps.build.outputs.build_status }}';
            const testStatus = '${{ steps.tests.outputs.test_status }}';
            const timestamp = '${{ steps.build.outputs.build_timestamp }}';

            // Categorize the PR based on conventional commit-style prefixes
            let category = 'Other';
            const lowerTitle = prTitle.toLowerCase();
            if (lowerTitle.startsWith('feat')) category = 'Features';
            else if (lowerTitle.startsWith('fix')) category = 'Bug Fixes';
            else if (lowerTitle.startsWith('docs')) category = 'Documentation';
            else if (lowerTitle.startsWith('refactor')) category = 'Refactoring';
            else if (lowerTitle.startsWith('test')) category = 'Tests';
            else if (lowerTitle.startsWith('chore')) category = 'Chores';
            else if (lowerTitle.startsWith('perf')) category = 'Performance';

            const releaseEntry = {
              pr_number: parseInt(prNumber),
              title: prTitle,
              category: category,
              author: prAuthor,
              build_status: buildStatus,
              test_status: testStatus,
              timestamp: timestamp
            };

            // Write release notes entry as a build artifact (not to logs)
            const fs = require('fs');
            fs.mkdirSync('build-artifacts', { recursive: true });
            fs.writeFileSync(
              'build-artifacts/release-note-entry.json',
              JSON.stringify(releaseEntry, null, 2)
            );

            core.setOutput('category', category);

            // Log only non-sensitive summary
            core.info(`Release note entry created: [${category}] PR #${prNumber}`);

      - name: Upload release notes artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: release-notes-pr-${{ steps.pr_meta.outputs.pr_number }}
          path: build-artifacts/release-note-entry.json
          retention-days: 90

      - name: Post build status to PR
        if: always()
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const prNumber = parseInt('${{ steps.pr_meta.outputs.pr_number }}');
            const buildStatus = '${{ steps.build.outputs.build_status }}' || 'failure';
            const testStatus = '${{ steps.tests.outputs.test_status }}' || 'failure';
            const category = '${{ steps.release_notes.outputs.category }}' || 'Unknown';
            const timestamp = '${{ steps.build.outputs.build_timestamp }}' || 'N/A';
            const overallStatus = '${{ job.status }}';

            const statusEmoji = overallStatus === 'success' ? '✅' : '❌';
            const buildEmoji = buildStatus === 'success' ? '✅' : '❌';
            const testEmoji = testStatus === 'success' ? '✅' : '❌';

            const commentBody = [
              `## ${statusEmoji} Build Notification`,
              '',
              `| Check | Status |`,
              `|-------|--------|`,
              `| Build | ${buildEmoji} ${buildStatus} |`,
              `| Tests | ${testEmoji} ${testStatus} |`,
              '',
              `**Release Category:** ${category}`,
              `**Build Time:** ${timestamp}`,
              `**Run:** [${context.runId}](${context.serverUrl}/${context.repo.owner}/${context.repo.repo}/actions/runs/${context.runId})`,
              '',
              '---',
              '*This comment is automatically generated for release notes tracking.*'
            ].join('\n');

            // Find existing bot comment to update instead of creating duplicates
            const { data: comments } = await github.rest.issues.listComments({
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              issue_number: prNumber,
              per_page: 100
            });

            const botComment = comments.find(
              c => c.user.type === 'Bot' && c.body.includes('Build Notification')
            );

            if (botComment) {
              await github.rest.issues.updateComment({
                owner: context.repo.owner,
                repo: context.repo.repo,
                comment_id: botComment.id,
                body: commentBody
              });
              core.info(`Updated existing build notification comment ${botComment.id}`);
            } else {
              await github.rest.issues.createComment({
                owner: context.repo.owner,
                repo: context.repo.repo,
                issue_number: prNumber,
                body: commentBody
              });
              core.info('Created new build notification comment');
            }

      - name: Send webhook notification
        if: always()
        env:
          WEBHOOK_URL: ${{ secrets.BUILD_NOTIFICATION_WEBHOOK_URL }}
        run: |
          # Only send if webhook URL is configured
          if [ -z "$WEBHOOK_URL" ]; then
            echo "No webhook URL configured, skipping notification"
            exit 0
          fi

          # Build the JSON payload safely using jq to prevent injection
          PAYLOAD=$(jq -n \
            --arg pr_number "${{ steps.pr_meta.outputs.pr_number }}" \
            --arg pr_title "${{ steps.pr_meta.outputs.pr_title }}" \
            --arg pr_author "${{ steps.pr_meta.outputs.pr_author }}" \
            --arg category "${{ steps.release_notes.outputs.category }}" \
            --arg build_status "${{ steps.build.outputs.build_status }}" \
            --arg test_status "${{ steps.tests.outputs.test_status }}" \
            --arg overall_status "${{ job.status }}" \
            --arg run_url "${GITHUB_SERVER_URL}/${GITHUB_REPOSITORY}/actions/runs/${GITHUB_RUN_ID}" \
            --arg timestamp "${{ steps.build.outputs.build_timestamp }}" \
            '{
              pr_number: ($pr_number | tonumber),
              pr_title: $pr_title,
              pr_author: $pr_author,
              category: $category,
              build_status: $build_status,
              test_status: $test_status,
              overall_status: $overall_status,
              run_url: $run_url,
              timestamp: $timestamp
            }')

          # Send notification via webhook - URL is from secrets, not logged
          HTTP_STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
            --max-time 30 \
            --retry 2 \
            --retry-delay 5 \
            -X POST \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d "$PAYLOAD" \
            "$WEBHOOK_URL")

          if [ "$HTTP_STATUS" -ge 200 ] && [ "$HTTP_STATUS" -lt 300 ]; then
            echo "Webhook notification sent successfully (HTTP $HTTP_STATUS)"
          else
            echo "::warning::Webhook notification failed with HTTP status $HTTP_STATUS"
          fi
